paper_title: Analysis of Equipartition in a 1D Diatomic Chain (Inferred)

methods.method_name: Molecular Dynamics Simulation of 1D Diatomic Chain
methods.description: This method describes the numerical simulation procedure used to study the dynamics of a 1D diatomic chain, focusing on the approach to equipartition. It involves setting up initial conditions, integrating the equations of motion, and performing ensemble averaging.

steps.description: Set up initial conditions for the simulation runs. This involves choosing random phases uniformly distributed in [0, 2π] for each mode and assigning positive random initial energies E_k(0) under the constraint that the sum of energies over excited modes equals Nε. The highest 10% of frequency modes are initially excited.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize molecular dynamics simulation with random phases and energies for specified excited modes
outputs: Initial state (positions, momenta, or normal mode quantities) for each ensemble member

steps.description: Numerically integrate the equations of motion for the 1D diatomic chain using the eighth-order Yoshida algorithm with a typical time step ∆t = 0.1. The integration is performed for each set of initial conditions generated in Step 1.
steps.searchable_summary: perform numerical integration of equations of motion using Yoshida algorithm
inputs: Equations of motion for 1D diatomic chain
outputs: Trajectory data (time series of positions, momenta, or normal mode quantities) for each ensemble member

steps.description: Calculate relevant physical quantities from the simulation trajectory data, such as the energy of the kth normal mode E_k(t), the time average of E_k up to time T (Ē_k(T)) using a window parameter µ = 2/3, the spectral entropy S(t), and the normalized effective relative number of degrees of freedom ξ(t).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate time-averaged energy, spectral entropy, and equipartition measure from simulation trajectory
outputs: Calculated quantities over time (E_k(t), Ē_k(T), S(t), ξ(t)) for each ensemble member

steps.description: Perform an ensemble average of the numerical results obtained in Step 3 over the 120 different random choices of initial phases (and corresponding initial energies). This is done to suppress fluctuations and obtain statistically representative results.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ensemble averaging over multiple simulation runs
inputs: Calculated quantities from Step 3 for all ensemble members
outputs: Ensemble-averaged results (e.g., average ξ(t), average Ē_k(T))
